version_name: Product A Pilot - Approved Items Pending
outcome_instruction: Some questions ask what you have seen happen in and around your involvement. Your answers describe what you have observed — not proof that you caused it or that it has been independently confirmed.

items.item_text: I naturally notice when a community or relationship seems to be drifting away from what faithfulness to Jesus requires.
items.prompt_text: I naturally notice when a community or relationship seems to be drifting away from what faithfulness to Jesus requires.
items.confound_controlled: Chronic critics; pair with PP-007 shadow

items.item_text: When I see something concerning about a community's direction, I feel a weight to name it — even when I would rather stay quiet.
items.prompt_text: When I see something concerning about a community's direction, I feel a weight to name it — even when I would rather stay quiet.
items.confound_controlled: Instinct claimed without behavioral follow-through; pair with PP-005

items.item_text: In the past 12 months, how often did you return to specific Scripture passages to test a strong impression or concern before acting on it?
items.prompt_text: In the past 12 months, how often did you return to specific Scripture passages to test a strong impression or concern before acting on it?
items.confound_controlled: Self-report inflation; pair with PP-010

items.item_text: In the past three years, I can identify a specific time when I named something I believed needed to be said, even though it carried a real relational, reputational, or ministry cost.
items.prompt_text: In the past three years, I can identify a specific time when I named something I believed needed to be said, even though it carried a real relational, reputational, or ministry cost.

items.confound_controlled: Core anchor; cost specificity guards against comfortable truth-telling claims
items.cultural_note: Works across formal/informal; does not require public ministry

items.item_text: When I see something that concerns me, I tend to say what I think directly without much concern for how it lands.
items.prompt_text: When I see something that concerns me, I tend to say what I think directly without much concern for how it lands.
items.confound_controlled: Strong agree = delivery-without-accountability pattern

items.prompt_text: In the past 12 months, I can identify a specific time when I named a pattern of drift in a community — not just a single incident — and said what I saw.

I can identify one concrete example

items.item_text: A practice in your community — around finances, relationships, or conflict — has become well-established and valued by most. You have begun to wonder whether it actually reflects what Jesus taught. The evidence from Scripture is not immediately clear. What most naturally describes your response? (A) Study the relevant Scripture passages carefully before saying anything; (B) Talk privately with the people most affected and find out what they are experiencing; (C) Name your concern provisionally to a trusted person or small group and ask them to help you test whether you are seeing clearly; (D) Keep watching the pattern closely to gather more evidence before deciding whether it is worth raising.
items.prompt_text: A practice in your community — around finances, relationships, or conflict — has become well-established and valued by most. You have begun to wonder whether it actually reflects what Jesus taught. The evidence from Scripture is not immediately clear. What most naturally describes your response?

options.option_text: Study the relevant Scripture passages carefully before saying anything
Talk privately with the people most affected and find out what they are experiencing
Name your concern provisionally to a trusted person or small group and ask them to help you test whether you are seeing clearly
Keep watching the pattern closely to gather more evidence before deciding whether it is worth raising.

items.intended_signal: PL-3: C = strongest Prophetic signal (provisional naming with humility + testing). A = TE-1 (study first). B = SH-1 (pastoral attention). D = cautious observation (wisdom or avoidance).
items.confound_controlled: No single obviously correct answer; A and C are both reasonable; cross-loading with TE-1 expected; flag for cognitive interview
items.cultural_note: Works across contexts; no institution required

items.item_text: You have been in a relationship with someone who doesn't know Jesus for about a year. They have been curious and open but have not shown readiness to follow him. What most naturally describes your approach? (A) Continue building the relationship and trust the Spirit's timing; (B) Find a natural opportunity to make a clear, direct invitation; (C) Introduce them to others who might also share the gospel with them; (D) Pray more intentionally and wait for a clearer sign of openness.
items.prompt_text: You have been in a relationship with someone who doesn't know Jesus for about a year. They have been curious and open but have not shown readiness to follow him. What most naturally describes your approach?

options.option_text: Continue building the relationship and trust the Spirit's timing
Find a natural opportunity to make a clear, direct invitation
Introduce them to others who might also share the gospel with them
Pray more intentionally and wait for a clearer sign of openness.

items.intended_signal: B = active EV-3 invitation. A = patience (may be appropriate or avoidance). C = network-referral (EV-4/Apostolic). D = passivity or spiritual attentiveness.
items.confound_controlled: Best-balanced scenario in bank; no single obviously correct answer

items.item_text: Someone you care about is about to make a decision that will cause them real harm. You have shared your concern clearly and they have chosen to proceed. What most naturally describes your response? (A) Respect their decision and remain present in the relationship; (B) Express your concern once more and ask them to reconsider; (C) Take action to prevent the outcome, even without their agreement; (D) Feel very anxious and find it genuinely hard to accept you cannot change this.
items.prompt_text: Someone you care about is about to make a decision that will cause them real harm. You have shared your concern clearly and they have chosen to proceed. What most naturally describes your response?

options.option_text: Respect their decision and remain present in the relationship
Express your concern once more and ask them to reconsider
Take action to prevent the outcome, even without their agreement
Feel very anxious and find it genuinely hard to accept you cannot change this.

items.intended_signal: A = mature SH-2 (respects agency). C = shadow (overriding agency). D = enmeshment/anxiety signal. B = partial — one further expression without escalation.
items.confound_controlled: "Real harm" must read as genuine; option D may be misread as generally emotional; flag for cognitive interview

items.item_text: In the past three years, I can identify a time I protected someone from accountability or consequences that would have been appropriate — because I found it hard to let them face that difficulty.

items.item_text: A respected person in your community has shared a teaching from a familiar passage that you believe misses what the text actually means. The teaching is not dangerous but could leave people with a limited understanding. What most naturally describes your response? (A) Go back to the passage yourself, study the context carefully, and work out what you think it means; (B) Ask the person privately how they arrived at their interpretation and share what you see in the text; (C) Bring the passage up in your next group discussion and invite everyone to examine it together; (D) Wait to see whether the teaching causes any confusion before deciding whether to address it.
items.prompt_text: A respected person in your community has shared a teaching from a familiar passage that you believe misses what the text actually means. The teaching is not dangerous but could leave people with a limited understanding. What most naturally describes your response?

options.option_text: Go back to the passage yourself, study the context carefully, and work out what you think it means
Ask the person privately how they arrived at their interpretation and share what you see in the text
Bring the passage up in your next group discussion and invite everyone to examine it together
Wait to see whether the teaching causes any confusion before deciding whether to address it.

items.intended_signal: A = TE-1 (careful study before acting). B = TE-3 (relational correction). C = TE-2 (communal text examination). D = pastoral caution / wait-and-see. No option is cartoonishly poor; A+B is the mature TE sequence but neither alone is wrong.
items.confound_controlled: Cross-loads with PL-3 (Prophetic may also name); flag for cognitive interview — distinguish by manner and preparation

I can identify more than one concrete example
Others could readily confirm this pattern

items.cultural_note: Requires respondent to have taught someone in past year

items.item_text: I find it difficult to stay focused on one thing long enough to see it become stable — I feel the pull toward the next new possibility before the current one is established.
items.prompt_text: I find it difficult to stay focused on one thing long enough to see it become stable — I feel the pull toward the next new possibility before the current one is established.

items.item_text: You have identified what looks like a significant opportunity for the gospel to take root in a specific place or network. You have some knowledge of the situation but not full clarity. No one else in your community is ready to commit. What most naturally describes your response? (A) Begin a small, reversible experiment — a conversation, a visit, a connection — and invite others to observe or join as it develops; (B) Keep building your understanding of the people and context before taking any first step; (C) Bring the vision to an existing leader or community and ask them to sponsor or support the first step; (D) Wait until you have at least one other committed partner who shares your sense of the opportunity.
items.prompt_text: You have identified what looks like a significant opportunity for the gospel to take root in a specific place or network. You have some knowledge of the situation but not full clarity. No one else in your community is ready to commit. What most naturally describes your response?

options.option_text: Begin a small, reversible experiment — a conversation, a visit, a connection — and invite others to observe or join as it develops
Keep building your understanding of the people and context before taking any first step
Bring the vision to an existing leader or community and ask them to sponsor or support the first step
Wait until you have at least one other committed partner who shares your sense of the opportunity.

items.intended_signal: A = FI-1 Apostolic (proportionate small experiment with invitation). B = FI-3/wisdom (deepen understanding first). C = EC-1/relational (route through existing structure). D = consensus-building (SH-4 or community readiness instinct). No option is cartoonishly wrong; A is the Apostolic signal but is modest and learning-oriented.
items.confound_controlled: Flag for practitioner review: test whether respondents read A as immediate unilateral action or as the proportionate experiment intended
items.cultural_note: Avoid implying international or high-resource context

items.item_text: Before I move forward on a significant kingdom initiative, I bring it to people I trust for honest input — not to get permission, but to test whether I am seeing clearly.
items.prompt_text: Before I move forward on a significant kingdom initiative, I bring it to people I trust for honest input — not to get permission, but to test whether I am seeing clearly.
items.confound_controlled: Critical maturity marker; pairs with PAD-001 perception and PAF-011 shadow

items.prompt_text: In the past three years, I can identify a significant thing I started that I moved away from before it was stable — and others had to carry forward what I began.

I can identify one concrete example
I can identify more than one concrete example

items.intended_signal: Shadow: novelty-and-abandonment; serial initiation without establishment
items.confound_controlled: Strong agree = critical Apostolic shadow; pairs with PAD-007
items.cultural_note: Low SD; important for Apostolic integrity

items.item_text: In the past 12 months, I took a concrete step toward a person, network, or place with little meaningful gospel access — including nearby immigrant, workplace, neighborhood, campus, or other networks.
items.prompt_text: In the past 12 months, I took a concrete step toward a person, network, or place with little meaningful gospel access — including nearby immigrant, workplace, neighborhood, campus, or other networks.

I can identify one concrete example

items.item_text: I find that I genuinely want to know God — not just know about him — and this desire exists apart from what it produces in ministry.
items.prompt_text: I find that I genuinely want to know God — not just know about him — and this desire exists apart from what it produces in ministry.
items.intended_signal: Hunger for God: desire beyond ministry outcomes
items.confound_controlled: Very High SD; requires PAF-002 behavioral and PAF-003 shadow

items.item_text: In the past 12 months, I can identify a sustained period when personal prayer and Scripture remained important to me even when ministry activity or visible results were limited.
items.prompt_text: In the past 12 months, I can identify a sustained period when personal prayer and Scripture remained important to me even when ministry activity or visible results were limited.

options.option_text: I can identify a weak or unclear example
I can identify one concrete example
I can identify more than one concrete example

items.confound_controlled: Core anchor; "when results were limited" tests genuine desire vs. duty

items.item_text: My sense of connection with God and sense of his direction are primarily expressed through what I am doing for him — my ministry activity and work.
items.prompt_text: My sense of connection with God and sense of his direction are primarily expressed through what I am doing for him — my ministry activity and work.
items.intended_signal: Shadow: ministry displacement of prayer life
items.confound_controlled: Strong agree = SD-1 shadow (doing-for-God replaces being-with-God)
items.cultural_note: Low SD; important for Apostolic spiritual integrity

items.item_text: When I sense that God may be showing me something, I feel a pull toward testing that sense rather than immediately acting on it.
items.prompt_text: When I sense that God may be showing me something, I feel a pull toward testing that sense rather than immediately acting on it.
items.confound_controlled: Very High SD; pairs with PAF-005 behavioral and PAF-006 shadow
items.cultural_note: Works across charismatic and non-charismatic contexts

items.item_text: In the past three years, I can identify a time I held back from acting on a strong spiritual impression until I had tested it — and what I found through that process changed what I actually did.
items.prompt_text: In the past three years, I can identify a time I held back from acting on a strong spiritual impression until I had tested it — and what I found through that process changed what I actually did.

items.item_text: When I feel strongly that God is leading me somewhere, I trust that impression enough to act without checking it with others or testing it through Scripture.
items.prompt_text: When I feel strongly that God is leading me somewhere, I trust that impression enough to act without checking it with others or testing it through Scripture.
items.confound_controlled: Strong agree = SD-2 shadow; critical for Apostolic safety

items.item_text: In the past 12 months, how often did you pray for a specific named person, place, or community — not general prayer, but prayer with identifiable people or situations in mind?
items.prompt_text: In the past 12 months, how often did you pray for a specific named person, place, or community — not general prayer, but prayer with identifiable people or situations in mind?
items.confound_controlled: Specificity instruction guards against counting generic prayer

items.item_text: I believe that ordinary disciples — without theological training or ministry titles — can read, understand, and obey Scripture in ways that genuinely form their lives and those around them.
items.prompt_text: I believe that ordinary disciples — without theological training or ministry titles — can read, understand, and obey Scripture in ways that genuinely form their lives and those around them.
items.confound_controlled: Critical SD-4 marker; only item for this construct — mark as Limited

items.item_text: When I bring a specific situation before God in prayer, I genuinely expect that he can and may act — not just as a theological belief, but as a posture toward this particular situation.
items.prompt_text: When I bring a specific situation before God in prayer, I genuinely expect that he can and may act — not just as a theological belief, but as a posture toward this particular situation.
items.intended_signal: Expectant faith orientation: specific not generic
items.confound_controlled: Very High SD; specificity is construct requirement; pair with PAF-010 shadow

items.item_text: When I pray for something and it does not happen, I sometimes wonder whether the outcome reflects a failure of faith — mine or others'.
items.prompt_text: When I pray for something and it does not happen, I sometimes wonder whether the outcome reflects a failure of faith — mine or others'.

items.confound_controlled: Strong agree = SD-5 shadow; important pastoral safety item

items.prompt_text: When I feel strongly that something needs to happen, I tend to move forward even without clear communal discernment or confirmation from people I trust.

items.item_text: When I walk into a group or gathering, I find myself most naturally noticing: (A) who seems to be struggling or disconnected, or (B) who hasn't yet had meaningful access to the gospel.
items.prompt_text: When I walk into a group or gathering, I find myself most naturally noticing:

options.option_text: who seems to be struggling or disconnected, or
who hasn't yet had meaningful access to the gospel.

items.intended_signal: A = SH-1 care-attention. B = FI-2 frontier-attention.
items.confound_controlled: Distinguishes Shepherding from Apostolic social perception

items.item_text: When I think about my primary role in communities I am part of, I naturally gravitate toward: (A) starting new things and moving people toward a mission frontier, or (B) strengthening the care and mutual support of people already present.
items.prompt_text: When I think about my primary role in communities I am part of, I naturally gravitate toward:

options.option_text: starting new things and moving people toward a mission frontier, or
strengthening the care and mutual support of people already present.

items.intended_signal: A = FI-1 frontier energy. B = SH-4 community-building energy.
items.confound_controlled: Apostolic initiation vs. Shepherding consolidation

items.item_text: When I am helping someone work through a significant belief or question, I am more naturally drawn to: (A) understanding what they believe and helping them see the truth more clearly, or (B) staying in relationship long enough for genuine conviction to form through shared life.
items.prompt_text: When I am helping someone work through a significant belief or question, I am more naturally drawn to:

options.option_text: understanding what they believe and helping them see the truth more clearly, or
staying in relationship long enough for genuine conviction to form through shared life.

items.intended_signal: A = TE-2 clarity instinct. B = DT-1 sustained-relational conviction.
items.confound_controlled: Teaching vs. Apostolic Multiplying differentiation

items.item_text: I am more naturally drawn to noticing: (A) when a community is drifting from faithfulness to Jesus in how it lives, or (B) when a community is accepting beliefs or teachings not well-supported by Scripture.
items.prompt_text: I am more naturally drawn to noticing:

options.option_text: when a community is drifting from faithfulness to Jesus in how it lives, or
when a community is accepting beliefs or teachings not well-supported by Scripture.

items.intended_signal: A = PL-2 Prophetic. B = TE-3 Teaching.
items.confound_controlled: Prophetic faithfulness-direction vs. Teaching doctrinal-accuracy

items.item_text: When I think about the people I most want to reach with the gospel, I tend to focus on: (A) people with little or no meaningful access to the gospel, or (B) people in my existing relational network who have not yet followed Jesus.
items.prompt_text: When I think about the people I most want to reach with the gospel, I tend to focus on:

options.option_text: people with little or no meaningful access to the gospel, or
people in my existing relational network who have not yet followed Jesus.

items.intended_signal: A = FI-2 Apostolic frontier. B = EV-2 Evangelistic proximity.
items.confound_controlled: Apostolic frontier vs. Evangelistic relational-network
items.cultural_note: Wording review needed to avoid implying international missions only

items.item_text: When I approach a complex situation or question, I am more naturally drawn to: (A) organizing what Scripture says and how it fits together, or (B) identifying what needs to happen and who needs to make it possible.
items.prompt_text: When I approach a complex situation or question, I am more naturally drawn to:

options.option_text: organizing what Scripture says and how it fits together, or
identifying what needs to happen and who needs to make it possible.

items.intended_signal: A = TE-1 systematic-Scripture thinking. B = FI-1/EC-1 mission-organization thinking.
items.confound_controlled: Teaching systematic vs. Apostolic mission-organization cognitive style

items.item_text: When someone needs both truth and care — where the true thing might hurt them — I tend to: (A) lead with the truth and trust the care will follow, or (B) lead with the care and trust the right moment for truth will emerge.
items.prompt_text: When someone needs both truth and care — where the true thing might hurt them — I tend to:

options.option_text: lead with the truth and trust the care will follow, or
lead with the care and trust the right moment for truth will emerge.

items.intended_signal: A = PL-3 truth-first instinct. B = SH-2/SH-3 care-first instinct.
items.confound_controlled: Prophetic vs. Shepherding under pressure to speak
items.cultural_note: Works broadly; assumes respondent values both

items.item_text: When I invest in another person's growth, I am more energized by: (A) helping them be able to share the gospel with the people in their life, or (B) helping them engage Scripture faithfully for themselves.
items.prompt_text: When I invest in another person's growth, I am more energized by:

options.option_text: helping them be able to share the gospel with the people in their life, or
helping them engage Scripture faithfully for themselves.

items.intended_signal: A = EV-4 witness-multiplication. B = TE-4 Scripture-formation.
items.confound_controlled: Evangelistic vs. Teaching equipping instinct
